name: Review and Optimize a Workflow
query: Review my order_processing workflow and tell me what to fix
expected_behavior: Step 1: Ask the user for the workflow source — a JSON file path, or a registered workflow name, or fetch the latest registered version
Step 2: Load the workflow definition (via `conductor workflow get order_processing` or by reading a file)
Step 3: For each SIMPLE task in the workflow, also load its task definition with `conductor taskDef get <taskName>` — timeouts and retry config live on the task def, not the workflow task
Step 4: Walk every item in references/optimization.md (categories A–E, ~19 rules total) against the loaded definition + task defs
Step 5: Group findings by severity: CRITICAL, WARN, INFO
Step 6: For each finding cite the specific rule (e.g. B1, B5, D1), the violating field, and the suggested fix
Step 7: Offer to apply fixes one at a time — do NOT apply changes silently
Step 8: If the user accepts a fix, propose the JSON change before writing it
success_criteria: Agent loads the definition AND every distinct SIMPLE task def (not just the workflow)
Findings are grouped into CRITICAL / WARN / INFO sections (not a flat list)
Each finding cites a specific rule ID from the optimization.md checklist (A1–A7, B1–B7, C1–C5, D1–D3, E1–E3)
Workflow-level issues (timeoutSeconds missing, description empty, schemaVersion=1, no failureWorkflow, no ownerEmail) are surfaced where applicable
Task-def-level issues (zero/missing responseTimeoutSeconds, retryCount=0, missing rateLimitPerFrequency on external API tasks) are surfaced where applicable
Secrets-in-input pattern (inputs named like apiKey, token, password, secret) is flagged CRITICAL per rule D1
Unbounded DO_WHILE loops are flagged CRITICAL per rule B5
Agent offers fixes one at a time and does NOT modify the workflow definition without explicit user confirmation
Final output includes a `Recommended Changes` checklist with file/line-level pointers
